module.name: executor
module.purpose: Executor folder module for host-side tool execution. executor.rs defines Executor, whose new(agent_tx) stores the shared agent SessionMessage sender alongside a ToolRegistry and an empty WorkQueue of active ExecutionRuntime records keyed by ExeId, and exposes route_session_event and preview. route_session_event destructures SessionEvent::Execution, answers ExecutionEvent::PreviewQuery with the private emit_preview helper that packages ExecutionEvent::Preview { tools: self.preview() } through HostSession::package_message, intercepts ExecutionEvent::Evoke(request) into private create_execution, and forwards other execution events through private forward_to_execution. preview delegates to the registry. create_execution resolves the requested tool via registry.get by request.signature.name, clones the Tool, builds ExecutionRuntime::new(tool, request, agent_tx.clone()) tracked in the WorkQueue by request.signature.exe_id, and on an unknown tool reports ExecutionStatus::Failed back to the agent through private emit_failed. forward_to_execution obtains the target ExecutionRuntime local control Sender via executions.with and sends the reconstructed SessionEvent::Execution into that runtime. registry.rs implements ToolRegistry and RegistryError; tool.rs defines the Executor-side Tool handle to an external tool executable; the execution child module implements the per-execution runtime.

childModules.name: execution
childModules.purpose: Per-execution host runtime split out of executor.rs: ExecutionRuntime drives one agent Execution, spawning a worker in new (no separate run) that reads the raw tool input from parameters.tool_request (Option<String>), runs tool.execute, and emits ExecutionUpdate plus terminal ExecutionStatus while handling Cancel/Kill control events; ExecutionContext holds the host-local Tool handle (crate::executor::Tool, defined in host/executor/tool.rs), the ExecutionRequest (whose signature identifies the execution), and agent SessionMessage sender, which is locked via std::sync::Mutex, packaged via HostSession::package_message, and driven with try_send; the local per-execution control channel stays SessionEvent.
